content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array AAA, print all possible **non-empty** subsets of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN - size of array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output M+1M+1M+1 lines, where MMM is the number of subsets:



The first line contains a single integer MMM.
The next MMM lines contains a subset of AAA.

Note:



Elements in the subset must be in non-decreasing order.
Subsets must not be duplicated.
The subsets must be sorted lexicographically. Subset XXX is said to be lexicographically smaller than subset YYY if either XXX is a prefix of YYY or there exists an index iii such that for all j<ij \lt ij<i, Xj=YjX_j = Y_jXj​=Yj​ and Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 303030.
